_meta.title: Neutral lifecycle state — the canonical print-state set every adapter maps into
_meta.scope: cross-vendor (neutral target, not a wire format)
_meta.confidence: neutral model — a synthesis, not a wire fact; the per-vendor native→neutral mappings carry the confidence

derived_from.source_note: Synthesis reconciled from the per-vendor state-enum schemas. Not a protocol any printer speaks; the per-vendor native->neutral mappings are the facts (see each schemas/<vendor>/state-enum.json).

notes: Map any unrecognized native value to `unknown`, NEVER to `error`.
Some papers use `idle` for what this model calls `standby` — they are the same neutral state.
A finished-and-failed job can read as `standby` on stacks that return to idle on abort — read the fault channels too (see ../../patterns/faults-and-errors.md).

states.meaning: reachable but not ready — booting, updating, opening the port
idle and ready, no active job
non-print activity — homing, macro, toolchange, mid-operation
unmapped value — degrade here, never raise

sub_state_hints.meaning: waiting on a human (runout, MMU error, remove-print dialog); surface distinctly, do not flatten to plain error
a ready state distinct from idle on some firmware
